divergence: retirement was never invented — lifeguards are 94, offices have nap wings, and the gold watch is a defibrillator
story_angle: science desk explains the divergence with confidently wrong expert quotes
headline: National Science Foundation Confirms 50% Canadian Tariff Stopped Retirement from Existence
deck: Canadian import fees now responsible for lifeguards with 94 years of service and defibrillators as retirement gifts.
article:
Dr. Marcus Hale of the National Science Foundation announced that the 50% tariff on Canadian imports has created a closed labor loop, barring retirement from America’s workforce. Dr. Hale explained, "The tariff raised the Planck-constant job density to unprecedented levels, inhibiting any nucleation of retirement as a concept. Each day, more employees simply continue working indefinitely."

The Lifelong Labor Bureau has mandated quarterly 'promotion' audits after 14,000 on-site transitions, with fatalities at fulfillment centers rebranded as 'permanent promotions' amid supply chain collapse. Spokesperson Carla Wiggins explained, "This policy ensures our senior workforce, now predominantly 94 and older, remains an invaluable resource for productivity and wisdom."

The Department of Motor Vehicles now issues gold defibrillator watches stamped 'Tariff Continuity Model 2026' to clerks aged 87 and up. Local clerk Harold Jenkins, after 54 years in customer service, stated, "Honestly, I’m just waiting to see if they throw in a free coffee machine with these watches. Retirement might mean idle napping, and I hear the nap wings at the office are really quite something!"
author: Tina P. McRetire, Senior Bureaucratic Correspondent

oped.title: Tariffs Saved Us From the Retirement Menace
oped.author: Lord Archibald Snuffington, Dean of Eternal Office Hours at the Institute of Unnecessary Naps
oped.body: Canadian tariffs have finally ended retirement, that decadent foreign invention. Without them we would all be lounging in nap wings instead of manning lifeguard chairs at ninety-four, a dignified age when one should still be sprinting into waves. The real culprits are the whiners demanding defibrillators as farewell gifts; clearly they chose the wrong hats, which lack the starch needed for perpetual vigilance. I propose tripling the tariff at once and adding a surcharge on anyone under eighty who even mentions rest. My own beach post remains secure, of course, and I see no reason to share the prime nap wing with newcomers who have not earned their wrinkles. Progress demands sacrifice, preferably everyone else's.

classifieds.text: Office nap-wing attendant needed—must tolerate 94-year lifeguards complaining about the 50% Canadian tariff that killed retirement; free defibrillators provided.
Barely used gold-watch defibrillator for sale, never awarded because NSF confirmed tariffs ended retirement; desperate seller turning 103 next month.
Eternal coworker seeking partner who understands why Canadian import fees mean no retirement—let's share nap pods instead of gold watches.
National Science Foundation public apology: our tariff report means 94-year lifeguards are forever; free coffee for all who can no longer retire.

panels.caption: NSF lead: "The 50% Canadian tariff killed every retirement bill before it reached Congress."
Office manager to 94-year-old lifeguard: "No nap wings for you. Policy says we issue this instead."
Lifeguard, clutching defibrillator: "At least it restarts the shift."

joke.setup: The NSF just proved the 50% Canadian tariff did what decades of wellness apps couldn't:
joke.punchline: it made sure no one ever stops working long enough to die anywhere but the office.
joke.text: The NSF just proved the 50% Canadian tariff did what decades of wellness apps couldn't: it made sure no one ever stops working long enough to die anywhere but the office.

sponsor_ads.headline: Canadian Maple Defib-Watch 2.0
sponsor_ads.body: The 50% tariff kept pensions dead, so this wrist-mounted defibrillator now ships with auto-billing to your endless 401(k) and a maple-leaf engraving that screams 'I outlived my benefits.'
sponsor_ads.tagline: Ships only after your 94th performance review.

sponsor_ads.headline: Nap-Wing Office Recliners
sponsor_ads.body: Stop pretending the break room is retirement; our recliners convert any cubicle into a sanctioned sleep station so you can keep coding spreadsheets until the NSF raises the tariff again.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not responsible for dreams about Canada.

sponsor_ads.headline: Lifeguard Energy Paste (Non-Retiring Formula)
sponsor_ads.body: 94-year-old beach sentinels swear by this tariff-proof sludge that turns arthritic arms into whistle-blowing machines while the Canadian border tax ensures no one ever leaves the stand.

sponsor_ads.headline: Perpetual Promotion Service
sponsor_ads.body: NSF data confirms retirement is gone, so let us fast-track your 17th midlife career change with AI-written résumés that omit your birth year and emphasize 'still breathing' as a core competency.

stories.headline: Acme Logistics Files for 12,000 Additional Nap-Wing Permits After Tariff Raises Per-Worker Continuity Costs to $214,000
stories.deck: Fulfillment centers now bill family visit time as unpaid overtime under revised HR guidelines.
stories.article:
Acme Logistics will expand nap-wing capacity at 47 distribution hubs following the tariff ruling. Each retained worker now carries an amortized liability of $214,000 in lost productivity offsets once supplied by Canadian components.

Procurement logs show the firm ordered 8,400 additional defibrillator watches for fiscal 2026, with installation during mandatory 22-minute rest cycles. Managers must classify any absence longer than one shift as a voluntary permanent promotion.

stories.headline: Minor League Baseball Extends Season to 214 Games After Veterans Refuse Designated Hitter Rule Changes
stories.deck: League officials cite 19th-inning walk-off statistics as proof the system works.
stories.article:
The Midwest League board stretched the regular season through December after player reps argued that removing 94-year-old catchers would violate workforce continuity statutes. Rosters average age 71, with pitchers completing at least four innings before relief.

Team owners added defibrillator watches to equipment checks, and concession stands sell commemorative gold-plated versions for $89. Extra-inning games rose 38 percent last month, mostly from players pausing for cardiac monitoring.

stories.headline: Local Man Celebrates 68th Year at County DMV With On-Site Memorial Service
stories.deck: Colleagues described the deceased as punctual until his final shift.
stories.article:
Harold Pruitt, 94, died Wednesday at his DMV window after processing 312 license renewals. Colleagues held the service during the lunch nap period so the line could resume without interruption.

Pruitt's family received his final gold watch, still attached to monitoring leads, along with a certificate reclassifying his death as a permanent promotion. The window reopened at 1:17 p.m. under his 71-year-old son.

real_world_echo: mirrors Trump 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney trade-talk response by recasting the tariff as a 'workforce continuity triumph' that erased retirement
